   591→# =============================================================================
   592→# Errors
   593→# =============================================================================
   594→
   595→class LogQLError(Exception):
   596→    def __init__(self, message: str, position: Optional[int] = None):
   597→        self.message = message
   598→        self.position = position
   599→        super().__init__(message)
   600→
   601→
   602→# =============================================================================
   603→# Parser
   604→# =============================================================================
   605→
   606→class Parser:
   607→    """Recursive-descent parser."""
   608→
   609→    def __init__(self, tokens: List[Token]):
   610→        self.tokens = tokens
   611→        self.pos = 0
   612→
   613→    def peek(self, offset: int = 0) -> Token:
   614→        p = self.pos + offset
   615→        if p >= len(self.tokens):
   616→            return self.tokens[-1]
   617→        return self.tokens[p]
   618→
   619→    def consume(self, expected_type: Optional[TokenType] = None,
   620→                expected_value: Optional[str] = None) -> Token:
   621→        t = self.peek()
   622→        if expected_type is not None and t.type != expected_type:
   623→            raise LogQLError(f"Expected {expected_type.name}, got {t.type.name}", t.position)
   624→        if expected_value is not None and t.value != expected_value:
   625→            raise LogQLError(f"Expected '{expected_value}', got '{t.value}'", t.position)
   626→        self.pos += 1
   627→        return t
   628→
   629→    def parse_query(self) -> Query:
   630→        self.consume(TokenType.SELECT)
   631→        select_list = self.parse_select_list()
   632→        self.consume(TokenType.FROM)
   633→
   634→        from_alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
   635→        if from_alias.value.lower() != "logs":
   636→            # Support any alias for FROM
   637→            pass
   638→
   639→        where_expr = None
   640→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.WHERE:
   641→            self.consume(TokenType.WHERE)
   642→            where_expr = self.parse_boolean_expr()
   643→
   644→        gloss_clause = None
   645→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.GLOSS:
   646→            self.consume(TokenType.GLOSS)
   647→            gloss_clause = self.parse_gloss_clause()
   648→
   649→        conflate_clauses = []
   650→        while self.peek().type == TokenType.CONFLATE:
   651→            self.consume(TokenType.CONFLATE)
   652→            clause = self.parse_conflate_clause()
   653→            conflate_clauses.append(clause)
   654→
   655→        group_by = None
   656→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.GROUP:
   657→            self.consume(TokenType.GROUP)
   658→            self.consume(TokenType.BY)
   659→            group_by = self.parse_group_list()
   660→
   661→        self.consume(TokenType.EOF)
   662→        return Query(select_list, where_expr, group_by, conflate_clauses, gloss_clause)
   663→
   664→    def parse_select_list(self) -> SelectList:
   665→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.STAR:
   666→            self.consume(TokenType.STAR)
   667→            return SelectList([], is_star=True)
   668→
   669→        fields = []
   670→        seen: Set[str] = set()
   671→        star_expansions = []
   672→
   673→        while True:
   674→            item = self.parse_select_item()
   675→
   676→            # Check for alias.* expansion
   677→            if item.field_ref and item.field_ref.key.endswith('.*'):
   678→                alias = item.field_ref.key[:-2]  # Remove ".*"
   679→                star_expansions.append(alias)
   680→                item.is_star_expansion = True
   681→
   682→            # For items with AS alias, use the alias for duplicate checking
   683→            output_key = item.output_key
   684→            if output_key in seen:
   685→                raise LogQLError(f"Duplicate output key '{output_key}'")
   686→            seen.add(output_key)
   687→
   688→            fields.append(item)
   689→
   690→            if self.peek().type != TokenType.COMMA:
   691→                break
   692→            self.consume(TokenType.COMMA)
   693→
   694→        return SelectList(fields, is_star=False, star_expansions=star_expansions)
   695→
   696→    def parse_field_ref(self) -> FieldRef:
   697→        parts = []
   698→        while self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENT:
   699→            ident = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
   700→            parts.append(ident.value)
   701→            if self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:
   702→                self.consume()  # consume '.'
   703→            else:
   704→                break
   705→        if not parts:
   706→            raise LogQLError("Expected identifier", self.peek().position)
   707→        return FieldRef(parts)
   708→
   709→    def parse_boolean_expr(self, min_precedence: int = 0) -> BooleanExpr:
   710→        # Parse left-hand side
   711→        left = self.parse_predicate_or_paren()
   712→
   713→        while True:
   714→            tok = self.peek()
   715→            if tok.type == TokenType.AND:
   716→                prec = 2
   717→            elif tok.type == TokenType.OR:
   718→                prec = 1
   719→            else:
   720→                break
   721→
   722→            if prec < min_precedence:
   723→                break
   724→
   725→            self.consume()
   726→            right = self.parse_boolean_expr(prec + 1)
   727→            left = BinaryExpr(op_token=tok, left=left, right=right)
   728→
   729→        return left

gloss_clause: Optional[GlossClause] = None  # GLOSS clause


@dataclass
class Conjunct:
    """A single equality predicate in a join condition."""
    left: FieldRef   # qualified field ref from one alias
    right: FieldRef  # qualified field ref from another alias


@dataclass
class JoinPred:
    """AND-only list of conjuncts."""
    conjuncts: List[Conjunct]


class JoinFlavor(Enum):
    INTERSECTING = "intersecting"  # inner join
    PRESERVING_LEFT = "preserving_left"  # left outer
    PRESERVING_RIGHT = "preserving_right"  # right outer
    PRESERVING_BOTH = "preserving_both"  # full outer


@dataclass
class ConflateClause:
    """A CONFLATE clause that joins another source."""
    alias: str              # alias to conflate with
    flavor: JoinFlavor      # join flavor (default INTERSECTING)
    join_pred: JoinPred     # join predicates


# =============================================================================
# Errors
# =============================================================================

class LogQLError(Exception):
    def __init__(self, message: str, position: Optional[int] = None):
        self.message = message
        self.position = position
        super().__init__(message)


# =============================================================================
# Parser
# =============================================================================

class Parser:
    """Recursive-descent parser."""

    def __init__(self, tokens: List[Token]):
        self.tokens = tokens
        self.pos = 0

    def peek(self, offset: int = 0) -> Token:
        p = self.pos + offset
        if p >= len(self.tokens):
            return self.tokens[-1]
        return self.tokens[p]

    def consume(self, expected_type: Optional[TokenType] = None,
                expected_value: Optional[str] = None) -> Token:
        t = self.peek()
        if expected_type is not None and t.type != expected_type:
            raise LogQLError(f"Expected {expected_type.name}, got {t.type.name}", t.position)
        if expected_value is not None and t.value != expected_value:
            raise LogQLError(f"Expected '{expected_value}', got '{t.value}'", t.position)
        self.pos += 1
        return t

    def parse_query(self) -> Query:
        self.consume(TokenType.SELECT)
        select_list = self.parse_select_list()
        self.consume(TokenType.FROM)

        from_alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
        if from_alias.value.lower() != "logs":
            # Support any alias for FROM
            pass

        where_expr = None
        if self.peek().type == TokenType.WHERE:
            self.consume(TokenType.WHERE)
            where_expr = self.parse_boolean_expr()

        gloss_clause = None
        if self.peek().type == TokenType.GLOSS:
            self.consume(TokenType.GLOSS)
            gloss_clause = self.parse_gloss_clause()

        conflate_clauses = []
        while self.peek().type == TokenType.CONFLATE:
            self.consume(TokenType.CONFLATE)
            clause = self.parse_conflate_clause()
            conflate_clauses.append(clause)

        group_by = None
        if self.peek().type == TokenType.GROUP:
            self.consume(TokenType.GROUP)
            self.consume(TokenType.BY)
            group_by = self.parse_group_list()

  1091→        self.consume(TokenType.LCURLY)

  1094→        while True:

  1096→            declarations.append(decl)

  1104→            raise LogQLError("Expected '}' closing GLOSS block", self.peek().position)

  1109→    def parse_group_list(self) -> List[FieldRef]:
  1110→        """Parse GROUP BY field list: <field_ref> (',' <field_ref>)*."""
  1111→        fields = []
  1112→        while True:
  1113→            # Could be a regular field ref or CANON.<name>

  1116→                ident = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
  1117→                parts.append(ident.value)
  1118→                if self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:

  1129→            fields.append(field_ref)
  1130→

  1133→            self.consume(TokenType.COMMA)

  1136→    def parse_select_item(self) -> SelectItem:
  1137→        """Parse a SELECT item: <field_ref> | <aggregate> [AS <ident>] | CANON.<name> [AS <ident>]."""
  1138→        # Check for CANON.<name>
  1139→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENT:
  1140→            ident = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
  1141→            if ident.value.lower() == "canon" and self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:

  1143→                self.consume(TokenType.DOT)
  1144→                name_tok = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
  1145→                canon_ref = CanonRef(name=name_tok.value)

  1149→                if self.peek().type == TokenType.AS:
  1150→                    self.consume(TokenType.AS)
  1151→                    as_alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT).value

  1154→                return SelectItem(
  1155→                    field_ref=None,
  1156→                    aggregate=None,
  1157→                    as_alias=as_alias,
  1158→                    is_star_expansion=False,
  1159→                    canon_ref=canon_ref
  1160→                )
  1161→            else:
  1162→                # It's a regular field reference - we already consumed the ident, so reconstruct
  1163→                # This is tricky since we already consumed the ident. Let me use the parse_field_ref approach.
  1164→                # Actually we need to rebuild - let me just create the field_ref from the consumed ident.
  1165→                field_ref = FieldRef(parts=[ident.value])

  1169→                    self.consume(TokenType.DOT)
  1170→                    more_idents = [ident.value]
  1171→                    while self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENT:

  1173→                        if self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:
  1174→                            self.consume(TokenType.DOT)

  1182→                    self.consume(TokenType.AS)
  1183→                    as_alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT).value

  1185→                return SelectItem(field_ref=field_ref, aggregate=None, as_alias=as_alias)

  1188→        agg_types = {

  1190→            TokenType.SUM: True,
  1191→            TokenType.AVG: True,
  1192→            TokenType.MIN: True,
  1193→            TokenType.MAX: True,
  1194→            TokenType.UNIQUE: True,

  1221→    def parse_field_ref_or_canon_in_aggregate(self) -> FieldRef:
  1222→        """Parse a field reference (or CANON reference represented as FieldRef) inside an aggregate."""

  1226→            if ident.value.lower() == "canon" and self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:
  1227→                self.consume(TokenType.DOT)
  1228→                name_tok = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
  1229→                return FieldRef(parts=["CANON", name_tok.value])
  1230→            else:
  1231→                # Not a CANON, re-construct the field ref
  1232→                parts = [ident.value]
  1233→                while self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:
  1234→                    self.consume(TokenType.DOT)
  1235→                    if self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENT:
  1236→                        parts.append(self.consume(TokenType.IDENT).value)

  1243→    def parse_conflate_clause(self) -> ConflateClause:
  1244→        """Parse a CONFLATE clause: <alias> [INTERSECTING|PRESERVING [LEFT|RIGHT|BOTH]] UPON <join_pred>."""
  1245→        alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
  1246→
  1247→        flavor = JoinFlavor.INTERSECTING  # default
  1248→
  1249→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.INTERSECTING:
  1250→            self.consume()
  1251→            flavor = JoinFlavor.INTERSECTING
  1252→        elif self.peek().type == TokenType.PRESERVING:
  1253→            self.consume()
  1254→            side = None
  1255→            if self.peek().type == TokenType.LEFT:
  1256→                side = "left"
  1257→                self.consume()
  1258→            elif self.peek().type == TokenType.RIGHT:
  1259→                side = "right"
  1260→                self.consume()
  1261→            elif self.peek().type == TokenType.BOTH:
  1262→                side = "both"
  1263→                self.consume()
  1264→
  1265→            if side == "left":
  1266→                flavor = JoinFlavor.PRESERVING_LEFT
  1267→            elif side == "right":
  1268→                flavor = JoinFlavor.PRESERVING_RIGHT
  1269→            elif side == "both":
  1270→                flavor = JoinFlavor.PRESERVING_BOTH
  1271→            else:
  1272→                flavor = JoinFlavor.PRESERVING_LEFT  # default to left if just PRESERVING
  1273→
  1274→        if self.peek().type != TokenType.UPON:
  1275→            raise LogQLError("Expected UPON in CONFLATE clause", self.peek().position)
  1276→        self.consume(TokenType.UPON)
  1277→
  1278→        conjuncts = []
  1279→        conjuncts.append(self.parse_conjunct())
  1280→
  1281→        while self.peek().type == TokenType.AND:
  1282→            self.consume(TokenType.AND)
  1283→            conjuncts.append(self.parse_conjunct())
  1284→
  1285→        join_pred = JoinPred(conjuncts=conjuncts)
  1286→        return ConflateClause(alias=alias.value, flavor=flavor, join_pred=join_pred)
  1287→
  1288→    def parse_conjunct(self) -> Conjunct:
  1289→        """Parse a single equality predicate in a join condition."""
  1290→        left = self.parse_field_ref_or_canon()
  1291→
  1292→        if self.peek().type != TokenType.OP_EQ:
  1293→            raise LogQLError("Expected '=' in UPON conjunct", self.peek().position)
  1294→        self.consume(TokenType.OP_EQ)
  1295→
  1296→        right = self.parse_field_ref_or_canon()
  1297→
  1298→        return Conjunct(left=left, right=right)

  1300→    def parse_field_ref_or_canon(self) -> FieldRef:
  1301→        """Parse either a FieldRef or a CANON reference (represented as FieldRef with parts ['CANON', <name>])."""
  1302→        # Check for CANON.<name>
  1303→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENT:
  1304→            ident = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
  1305→            if ident.value.lower() == "canon" and self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:
  1306→                self.consume(TokenType.DOT)
  1307→                name_tok = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
  1308→                return FieldRef(parts=["CANON", name_tok.value])
  1309→            else:
  1310→                # Not a CANON, re-construct the field ref
  1311→                return FieldRef(parts=[ident.value])
  1312→        elif self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:
  1313→            # Handle .something case
  1314→            self.consume(TokenType.DOT)
  1315→            ident = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
  1316→            return FieldRef(parts=["."] + [ident.value])
  1317→

  1320→    # Parse a <literal> for use in DEFAULT. Returns the parsed value.
  1321→    def parse_default_literal(self) -> Any:

  1326→def unescape_string(s: str) -> str:
  1327→    """Unescape \" and \\ in a string."""
  1328→    result = []
  1329→    i = 0
  1330→    while i < len(s):
  1331→        ch = s[i]
  1332→        if ch == '\\' and i + 1 < len(s):
  1333→            nxt = s[i + 1]
  1334→            if nxt == '"' or nxt == '\\':
  1335→                result.append(nxt)
  1336→                i += 2
  1337→            else:
  1338→                result.append(ch)
  1339→                i += 1
  1340→        else:
  1341→            result.append(ch)
  1342→            i += 1
  1343→    return "".join(result)
  1344→
  1345→
  1346→# =============================================================================
  1347→# Evaluation
  1348→# =============================================================================
  1349→
  1350→def get_nested(obj: Any, parts: List[str]) -> Any:
  1351→    """Traverse nested structure. Returns None if any intermediate is missing/invalid."""
  1352→    current = obj
  1353→    for part in parts:
  1354→        if not isinstance(current, dict):
  1355→            return None
  1356→        current = current.get(part)
  1357→        if current is None:
  1358→            return None
  1359→    return current
  1360→
  1361→
  1362→def compare_values(left: Any, op: Token, right: Any) -> bool:
  1363→    """Compare two values according to LogQL rules."""
  1364→    op_type = op.type
  1365→
  1366→    # Type mismatch: always false
  1367→    if type(left) != type(right) and not (left is None or right is None):
  1368→        # Special case: int and float can compare
  1369→        if not (isinstance(left, (int, float)) and isinstance(right, (int, float))):
  1370→            return False
  1371→
  1372→    # null handling
  1373→    if left is None or right is None:
  1374→        if op_type == TokenType.OP_EQ:
  1375→            return left is None and right is None
  1376→        elif op_type == TokenType.OP_NE:
  1377→            return not (left is None and right is None)
  1378→        else:
  1379→            # <, >, <=, >= with null are false
  1380→            return False
  1381→
  1382→    # Objects and arrays
  1383→    if isinstance(left, (dict, list)):
  1384→        if op_type == TokenType.OP_EQ:
  1385→            return left is right or left == right
  1386→        elif op_type == TokenType.OP_NE:
  1387→            return left != right
  1388→        else:
  1389→            return False
  1390→
  1391→    # Booleans - only = and != allowed
  1392→    if isinstance(left, bool):
  1393→        if op_type == TokenType.OP_EQ:
  1394→            return left == right
  1395→        elif op_type == TokenType.OP_NE:
  1396→            return left != right
  1397→        else:
  1398→            return False
  1399→

  1433→def evaluate(expr: BooleanExpr, record: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
  1434→    """Evaluate a boolean expression against a record."""
  1435→    if isinstance(expr, BinaryExpr):
  1436→        left_val = evaluate(expr.left, record)
  1437→        # Short-circuit evaluation
  1438→        if expr.op_token.type == TokenType.AND:
  1439→            if not left_val:
  1440→                return False
  1441→            return evaluate(expr.right, record)
  1442→        elif expr.op_token.type == TokenType.OR:
  1443→            if left_val:
  1444→                return True
  1445→            return evaluate(expr.right, record)
  1446→
  1447→    elif isinstance(expr, ParenExpr):
  1448→        return evaluate(expr.inner, record)
  1449→
  1450→    elif isinstance(expr, Predicate):
  1451→        field_val = get_nested(record, expr.field_ref.parts)
  1452→        return compare_values(field_val, expr.op_token, expr.literal)
  1453→
  1454→    return False
  1455→
  1456→
  1457→def project_record(record: Dict[str, Any], select_list: SelectList) -> Dict[str, Any]:
  1458→    """Extract selected fields from a record."""
  1459→    if select_list.is_star:
  1460→        return dict(record)
  1461→
  1462→    result = {}
  1463→    for field_ref in select_list.fields:
  1464→        result[field_ref.key] = get_nested(record, field_ref.parts)
  1465→    return result

  1468→# =============================================================================
  1469→# NDJSON Reader
  1470→# =============================================================================
  1471→
  1472→def read_ndjson(filepath: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
  1473→    """Read NDJSON file, ignoring blank lines."""
  1474→    records = []
  1475→    with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
  1476→        for line_num, line in enumerate(f, 1):
  1477→            line = line.rstrip('\n\r')
  1478→            # Skip blank lines and lines with only whitespace
  1479→            if not line or line.isspace():
  1480→                continue
  1481→            try:
  1482→                record = json.loads(line)
  1483→                records.append(record)
  1484→            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
  1485→                raise LogQLError(f"Invalid JSON at line {line_num}: {e}", line_num)
  1486→    return records
  1487→
  1488→
  1489→# =============================================================================
  1490→# Main
  1491→# =============================================================================
  1492→
  1493→def parse_args():
  1494→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
  1495→        description="LogQL - Query NDJSON logs with SQL-like syntax",
  1496→        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter
  1497→    )
  1498→    parser.add_argument("--query", required=True, help="SQL-like query string")
  1499→    parser.add_argument("--output", help="Write output to file instead of stdout")
  1500→    parser.add_argument("--log-file", help="Path to NDJSON file (shorthand for --source logs=<path>)")
  1501→    parser.add_argument("--source", action="append", help="Bind an alias to an NDJSON file (--source <alias>=<path>)")
  1502→    return parser.parse_args()
  1503→
  1504→
  1505→def main():
  1506→    args = parse_args()
  1507→
  1508→    try:
  1509→        # Build source alias mapping
  1510→        sources = {}
  1511→        if args.log_file:
  1512→            sources['logs'] = args.log_file
  1513→        if args.source:
  1514→            for src in args.source:
  1515→                if '=' not in src:
  1516→                    raise LogQLError(f"Invalid --source format: '{src}'. Expected <alias>=<path>")
  1517→                alias, path = src.split('=', 1)
  1518→                alias = alias.strip()
  1519→                path = path.strip()
  1520→                if not alias:
  1521→                    raise LogQLError("Alias cannot be empty")
  1522→                if not path:
  1523→                    raise LogQLError("Path cannot be empty")
  1524→                if alias in sources:
  1525→                    raise LogQLError(f"Duplicate alias binding: '{alias}'")
  1526→                sources[alias] = path
  1527→
  1528→        if not sources:
  1529→            raise LogQLError("At least one source is required (use --source or --log-file)")
  1530→
  1531→        # Tokenize
  1532→        tokenizer = Tokenizer(args.query)
  1533→        tokens = tokenizer.tokenize()
  1534→
  1535→        # Parse
  1536→        parser = Parser(tokens)
  1537→        query = parser.parse_query()
  1538→
  1539→        # Read all source files
  1540→        records = {}
  1541→        for alias, path in sources.items():
  1542→            records[alias] = read_ndjson(path)
  1543→
  1544→        # Filter and project
  1545→        results = []
  1546→        for record in records:
  1547→            if query.where_expr is None or evaluate(query.where_expr, record):
  1548→                results.append(project_record(record, query.select_list))
  1549→
  1550→        # Output
  1551→        output_json = json.dumps(results, ensure_ascii=False)
  1552→
  1553→        if args.output:
  1554→            with open(args.output, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
  1555→                f.write(output_json)
  1556→        else:
  1557→            print(output_json)
  1558→
  1559→    except LogQLError as e:
  1560→        error_obj = {"error": e.message}
  1561→        if e.position is not None:
  1562→            error_obj["position"] = e.position
  1563→        json.dump(error_obj, sys.stderr, ensure_ascii=False)
  1564→        sys.stderr.write("\n")
  1565→        sys.exit(1)
  1566→    except Exception as e:
  1567→        error_obj = {"error": str(e)}
  1568→        json.dump(error_obj, sys.stderr, ensure_ascii=False)
  1569→        sys.stderr.write("\n")
  1570→        sys.exit(1)
  1571→
  1572→
  1573→if __name__ == "__main__":
  1574→    main()

input.old_string:
def parse_query_inner(self) -> Query:
        """Parse a query (used for nested subqueries)."""
        self.consume(TokenType.SELECT)
        select_list = self.parse_select_list()
        self.consume(TokenType.FROM)

        from_alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
        if from_alias.value.lower() != "logs":
            # Support any alias for FROM
            pass

        where_expr = None
        if self.peek().type == TokenType.WHERE:
            self.consume(TokenType.WHERE)
            where_expr = self.parse_boolean_expr()

        gloss_clause = None
        if self.peek().type == TokenType.GLOSS:
            self.consume(TokenType.GLOSS)
            gloss_clause = self.parse_gloss_clause()

        conflate_clauses = []
        while self.peek().type == TokenType.CONFLATE:
            self.consume(TokenType.CONFLATE)
            clause = self.parse_conflate_clause()
            conflate_clauses.append(clause)

        group_by = None
        if self.peek().type == TokenType.GROUP:
            self.consume(TokenType.GROUP)
            self.consume(TokenType.BY)
            group_by = self.parse_group_list()

        return Query(select_list, where_expr, group_by, conflate_clauses, gloss_clause)

    def parse_select_item(self) -> SelectItem:
        """Parse a SELECT item: <field_ref> | <aggregate> [AS <ident>] | CANON.<name> [AS <ident>]."""
        # Check for CANON.<name>
        if self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENT:
            ident = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
            if ident.value.lower() == "canon" and self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:
                # Parse CANON.<name>
                self.consume(TokenType.DOT)
                name_tok = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
                canon_ref = CanonRef(name=name_tok.value)

                # Check for AS alias
                as_alias = None
                if self.peek().type == TokenType.AS:
                    self.consume(TokenType.AS)
                    as_alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT).value

                # For output, use CANON.name as key if no AS alias
                return SelectItem(
                    field_ref=None,
                    aggregate=None,
                    as_alias=as_alias,
                    is_star_expansion=False,
                    canon_ref=canon_ref
                )
            else:
                # It's a regular field reference - we already consumed the ident, so reconstruct
                # This is tricky since we already consumed the ident. Let me use the parse_field_ref approach.
                # Actually we need to rebuild - let me just create the field_ref from the consumed ident.
                field_ref = FieldRef(parts=[ident.value])

                # Check for AS alias or dot continuation
                if self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:
                    self.consume(TokenType.DOT)
                    more_idents = [ident.value]
                    while self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENT:
                        more_idents.append(self.consume(TokenType.IDENT).value)
                        if self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:
                            self.consume(TokenType.DOT)
                        else:
                            break
                    # Reconstruct field_ref with all parts
                    field_ref = FieldRef(parts=more_idents)

                as_alias = None
                if self.peek().type == TokenType.AS:
                    self.consume(TokenType.AS)
                    as_alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT).value

                return SelectItem(field_ref=field_ref, aggregate=None, as_alias=as_alias)

        # Check for POCKET subquery in SELECT list
        if self.peek().type == TokenType.POCKET:
            subquery = self.parse_subquery(is_table=False)
            as_alias = None
            if self.peek().type == TokenType.AS:
                self.consume(TokenType.AS)
                as_alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT).value
            return SelectItem(field_ref=None, aggregate=None, as_alias=as_alias,
                              scalar_subquery=ScalarSubqueryExpr(subquery=subquery))

        # Check for aggregate function
        agg_types = {
            TokenType.COUNT: True,
            TokenType.SUM: True,
            TokenType.AVG: True,
            TokenType.MIN: True,
            TokenType.MAX: True,
            TokenType.UNIQUE: True,
        }

        if self.peek().type in agg_types:
            func_token = self.peek()
            self.consume()
            if func_token.type == TokenType.COUNT:
                # COUNT(*)
                if self.peek().type == TokenType.STAR:
                    self.consume(TokenType.STAR)
                    agg = AggregateCall(func_token.type, None)
                else:
                    arg = self.parse_field_ref_or_canon_in_aggregate()
                    agg = AggregateCall(func_token.type, arg)
            else:
                arg = self.parse_field_ref_or_canon_in_aggregate()
                agg = AggregateCall(func_token.type, arg)

            as_alias = None
            if self.peek().type == TokenType.AS:
                self.consume(TokenType.AS)
                as_alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT).value

            return SelectItem(field_ref=None, aggregate=agg, as_alias=as_alias)

        raise LogQLError("Expected SELECT expression", self.peek().position)

                as_alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT).value

            return SelectItem(field_ref=None, aggregate=agg, as_alias=as_alias)

        raise LogQLError("Expected SELECT expression", self.peek().position)

    def parse_group_list(self) -> List[FieldRef]:
        """Parse GROUP BY field list: <field_ref> (',' <field_ref>)*."""
        fields = []
        while True:
            # Could be a regular field ref or CANON.<name>
            parts = []
            if self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENT:
                ident = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
                parts.append(ident.value)
                if self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:
                    self.consume(TokenType.DOT)
                    if self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENT:
                        ident = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
                        parts.append(ident.value)
                    else:
                        raise LogQLError("Expected identifier after '.'", self.peek().position)
            field_ref = FieldRef(parts)
            fields.append(field_ref)

            if self.peek().type != TokenType.COMMA:
                break
            self.consume(TokenType.COMMA)
        return fields

    def parse_select_item(self) -> SelectItem:
        """Parse a SELECT item: <field_ref> | <aggregate> [AS <ident>] | CANON.<name> [AS <ident>]."""
        # Check for CANON.<name>
        if self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENT:
            ident = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT)
            if ident.value.lower() == "canon" and self.peek().type == TokenType.DOT:

                    self.consume(TokenType.AS)
                    as_alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENT).value

    98→
    99→@dataclass
   100→class FieldRef:
   101→    parts: List[str]
   102→
   103→    @property
   104→    def key(self) -> str:
   105→        return ".".join(self.parts)
   106→
   107→    @property
   108→    def is_outer_ref(self) -> bool:
   109→        return len(self.parts) >= 2 and self.parts[0].lower() == "uptree"
   110→
   111→    def get_outer_alias(self) -> Optional[str]:
   112→        if self.is_outer_ref and len(self.parts) >= 2:
   113→            return self.parts[1]
   114→        return None
   115→
   116→    def get_field_path(self) -> List[str]:

   118→            return self.parts[2:]
   119→        return self.parts
   120→
   121→
   122→@dataclass

   124→    func: TokenType

   127→
   128→    @property
   129→    def canonical_key(self) -> str:
   130→        func_name = self.func.name

   132→            return f"{func_name}(*)"
   133→        return f"{func_name}({self.arg.key})"

   136→@dataclass
   137→class SelectItem:

   147→        if self.as_alias:

   150→            return self.aggregate.canonical_key
   151→        if self.canon_ref:
   152→            return self.canon_ref.key
   153→        if self.scalar_subquery:
   154→            return f"pocket_subquery"

   156→

   166→            refs.append(self.aggregate.arg)

   174→        refs = []

   186→        if isinstance(expr, (BinaryExpr, ExistsPredicate, InPredicate, AnyPredicate, AllPredicate)):
